title: Riverpod
objectives: Explain how Riverpod improves on Provider

sections.markdown: Riverpod is a reactive state + dependency-injection framework (by Provider's author) that fixes Provider's limits: it's compile-safe, not tied to the widget tree/BuildContext, and easy to test and compose.

Riverpod's wins over Provider: no BuildContext needed to read state, multiple providers of the same type, compile-time safety (no ProviderNotFound at runtime), and trivial overriding for tests. AsyncValue elegantly models loading/error/data.
Calling ref.watch inside callbacks (use ref.read) or doing side effects during build. Keep build pure; trigger actions via notifiers/ref.read.
Use code generation (@riverpod) for less boilerplate and auto-dispose. autoDispose frees provider state when no longer watched — important for memory.

flashcards.front: How does Riverpod improve on Provider?
flashcards.back: Compile-safe, no BuildContext needed, multiple same-type providers, easy testing/overrides.
A type modeling loading/error/data from Future/Stream providers, with .when().
watch subscribes/rebuilds in build; read is a one-off (use in callbacks).

quiz.q: A key Riverpod advantage over Provider is…
quiz.explain: Errors surface at compile time, not runtime.

quiz.q: In a callback you should use…

challenges.text: Fetch data with FutureProvider and render AsyncValue.when().
Build a NotifierProvider with complex state + actions.
Override providers with fakes in widget tests; verify behavior.

interviewQuestions: How does Riverpod improve on Provider?
